saida_bruta:
“Your face seems familiar,” he said courteously. “Weren’t you in the First Division during the war?”

“Yes, I was. I served in the Twenty-eighth Infantry.”

“I was with the Sixteenth until June of nineteen-eighteen. I knew I had seen you somewhere before.”

For a few minutes, we spoke about several damp, gray villages in France. He apparently lived nearby, because he told me he had recently bought a hydroplane and planned to test it the next morning.

“Would you like to come with me, old sport? We’ll stay close to the shore along the Sound.”

“What time?”

“Whenever it suits you.”

I was about to ask his name when Jordan turned toward us and smiled.

“Are you having a good time now?” she asked.

“Much better.” I turned back to my new acquaintance. “This is a rather unusual party for me. I haven’t even met the host. I live over there—” I gestured toward the invisible hedge in the distance, “and Gatsby sent his chauffeur over with an invitation.”

For a moment, he stared at me as though he did not understand.

“I’m Gatsby,” he said abruptly.

“What?” I cried. “Oh, forgive me.”

“I thought you knew, old sport. I’m afraid I’m not a very good host.”

“Much better.” I turned back to my new acquaintance. “This is an unusual sort of party for me. I haven’t even met the host. I live over there—” I gestured toward the invisible hedge in the distance, “and Gatsby sent his chauffeur to invite me.”

For a moment, he looked at me as though he had not understood.

“I’m Gatsby,” he said suddenly.

“What?” I exclaimed. “Oh, please forgive me.”

“I thought you knew, old sport. I’m afraid I’m not much of a host.

saida_bruta:
“Your face looks familiar,” he said politely. “Weren’t you in the First Division during the war?”

“Yes. I served with the Twenty-eighth Infantry.”

“I was with the Sixteenth until June 1918. I knew I’d seen you before.”

For several minutes, we talked about a few wet, gray villages in France. He lived nearby, he said, and had recently bought a hydroplane, which he intended to try out the following morning.

“Would you care to join me, old sport? We’ll keep close to shore along the Sound.”

“What time?”

“Whenever you please.”

I was about to ask his name when Jordan smiled and turned toward us.

“Are you enjoying yourself more now?” she asked.

“I’m Gatsby,” he said abruptly.

“What?” I cried. “Oh, please forgive me.”

“I thought you knew, old sport. I’m afraid I’m not much of a host.”
